description.value:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Stability: 0

an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.name: Purchased Traits
an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.description: You have 3 ancestry points to spend on the following traits.

description.value: As a maneuver, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 squares, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.
Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.
Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).
Edge on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: BeinmarksmÃ¥l

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

Skills: Religion/Lore, Insight, Medicine.

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 150

Perk: *True Omen*. Once per session, after you study signs or consult a rite, gain an edge on one test to interpret a supernatural or symbolic clue.

Whenever you fail a test to recall lore using a skill from the lore skill group, you instinctively recall the nearest location where the information you seek might be found. This could be the tower of a local sage, a library in a nearby city, somewhere deep in a dungeon, or any other location of the Directorâ€™s determination. The Director can decide that certain lore canâ€™t be revealed this way.

Demons and deathless fear you. Criminals run from the sight of your shadow. Agents of chaos, blasphemers, and heretics tremble at the sound of your voice. You carry the power of the gods, armed with wrath and sent out into the world first to seek, then censor those whose actionsâ€”or even existenceâ€”are anathema to your church.Â 

As a censor, youâ€™re at your best against the strongest foes. Your judgment terrifies heretics, stops enemies in their tracks, and even hurls them across the battlefield.

muZl4KsNLZbdcPMD.description: Choose a censor order from the following options, each of which grants you a skill. (Quick Build: Paragon.)

hVDXNXUF3DWZZmR4.name: Deity and Domains
hVDXNXUF3DWZZmR4.description:
Choose a god or saint who your character reveres from Chapter 14: Gods and Religion, or ask your Director about the deities in your campaign world. With the Directorâ€™s permission, you can also create your own deity and choose four domains to be part of their portfolio.

After choosing your deity, pick one domain from their portfolio. Your choice of domain determines many of the features youâ€™ll gain from this class. (Quick Build: Cavall as deity and War as domain.)

MWcvSkBGMFt6eLjv.description: The power you serve grants you a Heroic Resource called wrath, fueling your abilities as you censor those your church deems to be heretics.

ItQpJgCMQOLs0HZO.description: You utter a prayer that outlines your foe in holy energy.

A9K3KTrrkVl3i3Pk.name: Kit
A9K3KTrrkVl3i3Pk.description: You can use and gain the benefits of a kit. (Quick Build: Warrior Priest.)

UTko78iGyV9RWW5B.name: My Life for Yours
UTko78iGyV9RWW5B.description: You channel some of your vitality into more resilience for you or an ally.

VZJR8OYxF3Z40NPR.name: Signature Ability
VZJR8OYxF3Z40NPR.description: Choose one signature ability from the following options. Signature abilities can be used at will. (Quick Build: Your Allies Cannot Save You!)

QlwYpxf3WUAX2iee.description: The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you gain 2 wrath instead of 1.

SuC5lR7aOXOH1XmZ.description: Each time you finish a respite, you can choose one heroâ€™s weapon, including your own, to channel supernatural power as an implement of your godâ€™s wrath.

FnURQa5japiJN03y.name: Focused Wrath
FnURQa5japiJN03y.description: When you gain wrath at the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 3 wrath instead of 2.

qAwusw0yeIy8dIqf.name: 11 Wrath
qAwusw0yeIy8dIqf.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 11 wrath to use.

description.value: Without a strong example and a firm hand, the weak will be corrupted. You specialize in setting an example for your order. You have the Lead skill.

Lu6wUAmY7uFi55em.name: 2nd-Level Order Features
Lu6wUAmY7uFi55em.description: Your censor order grants you the following two features.

xuKGm9xQboZSb6JT.name: 2nd-Level Paragon Ability
xuKGm9xQboZSb6JT.description: Your censor order grants your choice of one of two heroic abilities.

WW4w1mhVw1mpMbYo.name: 5th-Level Paragon Feature
WW4w1mhVw1mpMbYo.description: Your censor order grants you a feature

5P0LRhfG03P7iQyY.name: 6th-Level Paragon Ability
5P0LRhfG03P7iQyY.description: Your censor order grants your choice of one of two heroic abilities.

Z3JS6QhJPrmaS6l4.name: 9th-Level Paragon Ability
Z3JS6QhJPrmaS6l4.description: Your censor order grants your choice of one of two heroic abilities.

description.value: The first time on a turn that you use your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.EhPpL42VZWxqeSEj]{Judgement} ability to judge a creature, you can vertical pull the judged creature up to a number of squares equal to [[lookup 2*@P evaluate=true]]{twice your Presence score}.

Your devotion to your deity allows you to take command of the battlefield, letting your allies benefit from your wisdom. While you are adjacent to a creature, your allies gain the benefits of flanking against that creature. Additionally, your allies gain an edge on tests made to aid other creatures with their tests.
You exhibit a small spark of your deityâ€™s power, causing creatures to trust or fear you, depending on what you need. You gain an edge on tests made to intimidate or persuade others.

system.story: The shock of your condemnation freezes your enemy in their boots.

effect.after: While the target is restrained this way, your abilities that impose forced movement can still move them.

description.value: Your divine spark grows in power, allowing you and your allies to focus and endure. At the start of each of your turns, you can spend 1d6 Stamina to end one effect on you that is ended by a saving throw or that ends at the end of your turn. Any ally who starts their turn within 5 squares of you can also spend Stamina to gain this benefit.

mZZObvCtykeSJ2bo.name: Domain Feature
mZZObvCtykeSJ2bo.description: You gain a domain feature from your domain, as shown on the 1st-Level Censor Domain Features table.

L0NjkyKvPtdsoNGV.name: 4th-Level Domain Feature
L0NjkyKvPtdsoNGV.description: You gain a domain feature from your domain, as shown on the 4th-Level Censor Domain Features table.

D38jKwVOufShVo7L.name: 7th-Level Domain Feature
D38jKwVOufShVo7L.description: You gain a domain feature from your domain

system.story: You craft objects with the power of your mind.

effect.before: You create a mundane object of size 1S or smaller. You can maintain a number of objects created this way equal to [[lookup @P]]{your Presence score}. You can destroy an object created this way with a thought, no matter how far you are from it (no action required).

description.value: When you use your Hands of the Maker ability, you can create a mundane object that is size 2 or smaller.

system.story: You utter a prayer that outlines your foe in holy energy.

effect.before:
The target is [[/apply MHQDlicbxXquwduc]]{judged} by you until the end of the encounter, you use this ability again, you willingly end this effect (no action required), or another censor judges the target.

Whenever a creature judged by you uses a main action and is within your line of effect, you can use a free triggered action to deal [[/damage 2*@P holy]]{holy damage} equal to twice your Presence score to them.

When a creature judged by you is reduced to 0 Stamina, you can use a free triggered action to use this ability against a new target. Additionally, you can spend 1 wrath to take one of the following free triggered actions:

  • When an adjacent creature judged by you starts to shift, you make a melee free strike against them and their speed becomes 0 until the end of the current turn, preventing them from shifting.

  • When a creature judged by you within 10 squares makes a power roll, you cause them to take a bane on the roll.

  • When a creature judged by you within 10 squares uses an ability with a potency that targets only one creature, the potency is reduced by 1 for that creature.

  • If you damage a creature judged by you with a melee ability, the creature is [[/apply taunted turn]] by you until the end of their next turn.

You can choose only one free triggered action option at a time, even if multiple options are triggered by the same effect.

description.value: The Spellsword kit combines melee strikes and a little bit of magic, letting you create a warrior who doesnâ€™t have to choose between the incantation and the blade.

system.story: Lightning jumps from your weapon as you strike to harm a nearby foe

effect.after: A creature or object of your choice within 2 squares of the target takes [[/damage @char lightning]]{lightning damage} equal to the characteristic score used for this abilityâ€™s power roll.

items.name: Look On My Work and Despair

description.value: Your judgment has grown in divine power, instilling fear in those you condemn. Whenever you use your Judgment ability, you can spend 1 wrath, and if the target has [[potency P average]], they are frightened of you (saveÂ ends). Additionally, whenever a creature judged by you is reduced to 0 Stamina and you use Judgment as a free triggered action, if the new target has [[potency P strong]], they are frightened of you (save ends). If the target is already frightened of you, they instead take [[/damage 2*@P]]{holy damage} equal to twice your Presence score.

system.story: You channel some of your vitality into more resilience for you or an ally
system.trigger: The target starts their turn or takes damage.

effect.before: You spend a Recovery and the target regains [[/heal @recoveries.recoveryValue]].

spend.text: You can end one effect on the target that is ended by a saving throw or that ends at the end of their turn, or a prone target can stand up.

description.value: Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.

On any day when you donâ€™t take a respite, you can spend 1 uninterrupted hour working on a crafting project using a skill you have from the crafting skill group. If you do so, you gain [[/r 1d10]] project points toward that project.

system.story: You show your foe a glimpse of their fate after death.

effect.after: Each time the target willingly moves before the end of your next turn, they take 1 psychic damage for each square they move.

description.value:
The power you serve grants you a Heroic Resource called wrath, fueling your abilities as you censor those your church deems to be heretics.

Wrath in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain wrath equal to [[lookup @hero.victories]]{your Victories}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you [[/gain 2 hr]]{gain 2 wrath}.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that a creature judged by you (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.EhPpL42VZWxqeSEj]{Judgement}) deals damage to you, you [[/gain 1 hr]]{gain 1 wrath}. The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you [[/gain 1 hr]]{gain 1 wrath}.

You lose any remaining wrath at the end of the encounter.

Wrath Outside of Combat

Though you canâ€™t gain wrath outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost wrath without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs wrath, you canâ€™t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Victories or finish a respite.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited wrath on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of wrath equal to [[lookup @hero.victories]]{your Victories}. (Such abilities arenâ€™t part of the core rules for the censor, but they might appear in future products.)
The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you gain 2 wrath instead of 1.

system.story: A mighty blow turns your foeâ€™s vitality into a holy light that envelops you and an ally, discouraging enemies who might attack you.

effect.after: Until the start of your next turn, enemies take a bane on ability rolls made against you or any ally adjacent to you.

system.story: "I got you, you son of a bitch.â€

effect.after: If the target makes a strike against a creature while grabbed this way, you can spend 3 wrath to deal [[/damage @P holy]]{holy damage} to them equal to your Presence score, then change the target of the strike to another target within the strikeâ€™s distance.

system.story: You shed a righteous energy that punishes enemies who would harm  you or your allies.

effect.before: Until the end of the encounter or until you are dying, whenever a target makes a strike, they take [[/damage 2*@P holy]]{holy damage} equal to twice your Presence score. A target judged by you takes an extra 2d6 holy damage. [[/damage 2d6+2*@P holy]]

system.story: You channel divine force for movement that cannot be stopped.

effect.before: The target ends any condition or effect on them that is ended by a saving throw or that ends at the end of their turn, or a prone target can stand up. The target then [[/surge 2]], can shift up to their speed while ignoring difficult terrain, and can use a strike signature ability as a free triggered action.
Choose an enemy adjacent to you. The next ability power roll an ally who makes against that creature before the start of your next turn has an edge.

A creature who uses the Catch Breath maneuver spends a Recovery and regains [[/heal @recoveries.recoveryValue]]{Stamina} equal to their recovery value.

A creature who is dying canâ€™t use the Catch Breath maneuver, but other creatures can help them spend Recoveries in other ways.

When a creature takes the Charge main action, they move up to their speed in a straight line without jumping, then make a @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.wU69Y06G9lYFrvp6]{melee free strike} against a target when they end their move. If the creature has an ability with the Charge keyword, they can use that ability against the target instead of a free strike.

A creature canâ€™t move through difficult terrain or shift when they charge. They can fly or burrow as part of the Charge main action if they have that movement available to them, but they canâ€™t climb or swim while charging unless they can automatically use that movement at full speed.

effects.name: Defending
effects.description: When a creature takes the Defend main action, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creatureâ€™s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.

effect.before: When a creature takes the [[/apply TYggdI2Kwa302NjW]]{Defend main action}, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creatureâ€™s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.
A creature who is grabbed by another creature, an object, or an effect (see Grab below) can attempt to escape by using the following ability.
effect.after: You take a bane on this maneuver if your size is smaller than the size of the creature, object, or effect that has you grabbed.

tier2.display: You can grab the target, but if you do, the target can make a melee free strike against you before they are grabbed

tier3.display: The target is grabbed by you.

effect.before: A creature seeking to keep a foe close and locked down can attempt to grab a creature using the following ability
effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature who uses the Heal main action employs medicine or inspiring words to make an adjacent creature feel better and stay in the fight. The target creature can spend a Recovery to regain Stamina, or can make a saving throw against one effect they are suffering that is ended by a saving throw.

effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature can use the Stand Up maneuver to stand up if they are prone, ending that condition. Alternatively, they can use this maneuver to make a willing adjacent prone creature stand up.
When a creature takes the Advance move action, they move a number of squares up to their speed. They can break up this movement with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

When a creature takes the Disengage move action, they can shift [[lookup @movement.disengage]]{1} square(s). Certain class features, kits, and other rules allow a creature to shift more than 1 square when they disengage. A creature who does so can break up their shift with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

A creature can take the Ride move action only while mounted on another creature. When a creature takes the Ride move action, they cause their mount to move up to the mountâ€™s speed, taking the rider with them. Alternatively, a creature can use the Ride move action to have their mount use the Disengage move action as a free triggered action. A creature can use the Ride move action only once per round. A mounted creature can only have this move action applied to them once per round. This movement can be broken up with the riderâ€™s maneuver and main action however they wish.

biography.value: ThrÃ¡inn Ashward is the commanding Inquisitor of the Pale Maw forces at Beinvaka. Tall and gaunt with a scar across one eye, he wears robes of deep purple with silver entropy symbols. His manner is clinical rather than fanatical â€” he has thought this through and is absolutely certain. He is the primary antagonist of Beats 16 and 18, commanding the forces that try to stop Kaelenâ€™s ritual.
biography.director:
BEATS 16 & 18 â€” Beinvaka & Surtâ€™s Forge

Pale Maw Inquisitor. He/him. Tall, gaunt, scar across one eye, deep purple robes. Clinical certainty â€” not fanaticism but absolute conviction.

BEAT 16 ULTIMATUM (opening):
â€œLeave the Keeper with us. You may go. We will let you pass. Or: if you refuse, we will take her â€” and you will all die. I am not making a threat. I am describing what will happen.â€

OPTIONS THE PARTY HAS:
(a) Combat (20â€“30+ cultists + Inquisitor â€” very likely a wipe).
(b) Mad Dash Montage â€” flee immediately. Does not require talking to ThrÃ¡inn at all.
(c) Negotiate with the Elder of Beinvaka (not with ThrÃ¡inn â€” he will not negotiate personally).

TACTIC IN BEAT 16: Waits. Does not attack first. Will attack if any hero makes an aggressive move. Will not stop the party if they attempt the Mad Dash â€” he orders pursuit instead.

IF KILLED IN BEAT 16: Yrsa Frostbane takes over in Beat 18. Opening: â€œThrÃ¡inn died speaking the truth. I will finish what he started.â€

IF ALIVE IN BEAT 18: Same stat block (see Pale Maw Inquisitor NPC monster file). Returns with 30 SP max if wounded but survived. Final words if defeated: â€œYou delay the inevitable. The Fimbulwinter is right. The world should end.â€

PALE MAW PHILOSOPHY (for social scenes): Entropy is not evil â€” it is correct. The Ever-Ember is not hope but defiance of natural order. Kaelen is not a hero but a heretic. â€œShe would chain the world to warmth it no longer deserves.â€
